rationale: The citing paper uses Li et al. (2022) for a specific methodological concern: repeated-question designs can shift decision processes and reduce external validity. This concern is directly tied to the citing paper's empirical strategy, where the authors report trial-order robustness checks and revisit the same issue in the limitations discussion. The citation therefore plays a meaningful role in framing and evaluating the credibility of the citing paper's repeated-trial design, not just general background.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Li et al. (2022)'s warning about external-validity risks from repeated elicitation to its own repeated-trial default-choice studies. It uses that prior insight to motivate robustness checks across trials and to qualify interpretation of its findings.
